content: {"analyses": [{"source_url": "https://www.mdpi.com/2073-4360/14/9/1721", "source_title": "Influence of Hydrothermal Aging under Two Typical Adhesives on Bonded Joints of BFRP Composites", "source_type": "journal_article", "source_quality": 0.72, "overall_relevance": 0.65, "year": 2022, "authors": [], "venue": "Polymers (MDPI)", "doi": "10.3390/polym14091721", "atomic_facts": [{"statement": "Tensile strength of adhesive joints decreased by approximately 31.3% after moisture absorption and by 54.4% under combined high temperature (95 C) and humid environment.", "direct_quote": "the tensile strength decreased by about 31.3% after moisture absorption, and decreased by 27.7% and 54.4% under high temperature (95 degrees C)/dry environment and high temperature (95 degrees C)/humid environment, respectively. Liu et al. studied the effect of high temperature curing epoxy adhesive on the tensile strength of joints under different aging environments", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.88, "confidence": 0.9, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["tensile strength", "moisture absorption", "epoxy adhesive", "95 C", "damp heat aging"]}, {"statement": "The saturated water absorption content of Araldite 2012 adhesive was approximately 14.05% and Araldite 2015 adhesive was approximately 16.03% under damp heat conditions of 80 C/95% RH.", "direct_quote": "the saturated water absorption content of A2012 adhesive is about 14.05%, and the saturated water absorption content of A2015 adhesive is about 16.03%. The moisture absorption characteristics of the adhesive sample are mainly affected by the damp and heat conditions, the thickness of the sample and the aging time", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.73, "confidence": 0.92, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["Araldite 2012", "Araldite 2015", "water absorption", "saturation", "80 C", "95% RH"]}, {"statement": "BFRP single lap joints were tested under two damp heat conditions: 80 C/95% RH and 80 C/pure water immersion at aging intervals of 0, 10, 20, and 30 days to determine load-displacement behavior.", "direct_quote": "The adhesive bodies (dumbbell specimens) were tested for water absorption under the two environments of 80 degrees C/95% RH and 80 degrees C/pure water, and they were analyzed by Fick's law. The damp and heat aging experiments of the BFRP-BFRP single lap joints under the above two environments were carried out, the load-displacement curves after different aging times (0 days, 10 days, 20 days, 30 days) were obtained", "fact_category": "methodology", "relevance_score": 0.71, "confidence": 0.93, "perspective": "Methodological", "entities": ["BFRP", "single lap joint", "80 C", "95% RH", "Fick's law", "damp heat aging"]}, {"statement": "During damp heat aging, higher temperature increases both the chemical reaction rate and the diffusion coefficient, promoting more water diffusion into the adhesive and further causing aging and degradation of the joint.", "direct_quote": "In the case of high temperature and high humidity, a higher temperature will increase the rate of the chemical reaction and the diffusion coefficient, promote more water diffusion, and further cause aging and degradation of the joint, thereby affecting the mechanical properties of the joint. Therefore, it is very important to study the degradation mechanism of composite materials", "fact_category": "causal_link", "relevance_score": 0.78, "confidence": 0.87, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["diffusion coefficient", "chemical reaction rate", "damp heat aging", "degradation mechanism"]}, {"statement": "When high temperature was combined with 90% relative humidity, shear strength of aluminum-steel adhesive joints was significantly affected after 60 days of exposure, while indoor humidity at various temperatures caused almost no change.", "direct_quote": "for indoor humidity and various temperatures, there was almost no change in shear strength after 60 days of exposure, but when high temperature and high humidity (90% relative humidity) were combined, the shear strength was significantly affected. Zhang et al. studied the shear strength of adhesive joints between thin aluminum and steel substrates at different temperatures", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.76, "confidence": 0.86, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["shear strength", "90% RH", "aluminum-steel joints", "60 days"]}, {"statement": "Effects of damp heat aging on fiber-reinforced composites include reversible effects mainly from moisture and irreversible effects such as reduction of interface strength, appearance of microstructure defects, and hydrolysis of the polymer matrix.", "direct_quote": "The effects of hot and humid environments on composite materials are usually divided into reversible effects (mainly due to moisture) and irreversible effects. Generally speaking, the absorbed water can be removed by drying the material, thereby eliminating the reversible effects, but the reduction of interface strength, the appearance of microstructure defects, and the hydrolysis of the polymer matrix are all irreversible", "fact_category": "causal_link", "relevance_score": 0.85, "confidence": 0.89, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["reversible effects", "irreversible effects", "hydrolysis", "interface strength", "microstructure defects", "polymer matrix"]}], "evidence_summary": "Comprehensive study on hydrothermal aging of BFRP adhesive joints showing 31-54% tensile strength reduction under damp heat, Fick's law moisture absorption characterization at 80C/95%RH, and distinction between reversible and irreversible aging effects."}, {"source_url": "https://repository.lboro.ac.uk/articles/journal_contribution/Degradation_of_interfacial_adhesion_strength_within_photovoltaic_mini-modules_during_damp-heat_exposure/9561614/files/17193764.pdf", "source_title": "Degradation of interfacial adhesion strength within photovoltaic mini-modules during damp-heat exposure", "source_type": "journal_article", "source_quality": 0.0, "overall_relevance": 0.0, "year": 0, "authors": [], "venue": "", "doi": "", "atomic_facts": [], "evidence_summary": "INSUFFICIENT_CONTENT: Source returned HTTP 403 Forbidden.
